title: Al Salam Educational Trust , Mumbai vs Ito, Exem Ward 1(1), Mumbai on 30 September, 2024
body: Al Salam Educational Trust , Mumbai vs Ito, Exem Ward 1(1), Mumbai on 30 September, 2024 IN THE INCOME TAX APPELLATE TRIBUNAL, 'A' BENCH MUMBAI BEFORE: SHRI AMIT SHUKLA, JUDICIAL MEMBER & SMT RENU JAUHRI, ACCOUNTANT MEMBER ITA No.3779 to 3782/Mum/2024 (Assessment Years :2018-19 to 2021-22) AL Salam Educational Vs. Income Tax Officer- Trust Exem Ward 1(1), 503, D Wing Mumbai Building No.2, Powai Vihar Complex, Powai, Mumbai-400 076 PAN/GIR No.AADTA9504C (Appellant) .. (Respondent) Assessee by Ms. Aasifa Khan Revenue by Shri Manoj Kumar Sinha Date of Hearing 05/09/2024 Date of Pronouncement 30/09/2024 आदे श / O R D E R PER AMIT SHUKLA (J.M): The aforesaid appeals have been filed by the assessee-trust against order dated 11/06/2024 passed by Addl./JCIT-Agra in relation to the intimation u/s.143(1) for the A.Yrs. 2018-19 to 2021-22. 2 ITA No.3779-3782/Mum/2024 AL Salam Educational Trust 2. In all the years the assessee is aggrieved by exparte order passed by the ld. CIT (A) denying the exemption u/s.11-12 by the CPC . 3. We will first take up the appeal for the A.Y.2018-19 and our finding given therein will apply mutatis mutandis. 4. The assessee is an educational trust established wholly for educational purposes for school children. The only activity carried out by the assessee trust was running of school for which it was granted registration u/s.12A . The CPC has denied exemption of Section 11 for all the years on the ground that the Form No.10B audit report has been filed beyond the due date. Before us ld. Counsel has filed following chronology of dates for all the assessment years. A.Y. 2018-19 CHRONOLOGY OF DATES Sr. No. Particulars Date 1. Due Date for filing Form No. 10B - 31/08/2018 Audit Report 2. Due Date for filing return of income 30/09/2018 u/s. 139(1) of the Act 3. Due Date extended for filing return of 31/10/2018 income as well as audit reports as per Instructions No. ENo.22 5/3 58/201 8/ITA.II dated 08/10/2018 4. Belated return filed u/s. 139(4) of the 14/03/2019 Act 5. Form No.10B dated 01/04/2019 e- 06/04/2019 filed on 3 ITA No.3779-3782/Mum/2024 AL Salam Educational Trust 6. No. of days of delay in filing the Form 156 days No.10B 7. Intimation order u/s. 143(1) of the Act 04//03/2020 8. CIT(A) order passed 11/06/2024 9. Application u/s.119(2)(b) of the Act to 09/08/2024 the CIT (Exemption) dated 05/08/2024 filed on 10. Application u/s.119(2)(b) of the Act to 20/08/2024 the CBDT dated 05/08/2024 e-mailed on A.Y. 2019-20 CHRONOLOGY OF DATES Sr. No. Particulars Date 1. Due Date for filing Form No. 10B - 31/08/2019 Audit Report 2. Due Date for filing return of income u/s. 30/09/2019 139(1) of the Act 3. Due Date extended for filing return of 31/10/2019 income as well as audit reports as per Instructions ENo. 225/157/201 9/ITA.II dated 27/09/20 19 4. Due Date for filing the return of income 30/11/2020 u/s. 139(4) extended as per Instructions ENo.225/150/2020- ITA-II dated 30/09/2020 4. Belated return filed u/s. 139(4) of the 29/09/2020 Act 5. Form No.10B dated 30/09/2020 e-filed 30/09/2020 on 4 ITA No.3779-3782/Mum/2024 AL Salam Educational Trust 6. No. of days of delay in filing the Form 332 days No.10B (from 01/11/2019 to 30/09/2020) 7. Intimation order u/s. 143(1) of the Act 11//02/2020 8. CIT(A) order passed 11/06/2024 9. Application u/s.119(2)(b) of the Act to 09/08/2024 the CIT (Exemption) dated 05/08/2024 filed on 10. Application u/s.119(2)(b) of the Act to 20/08/2024 the CBDT dated 05/08/2024 e-mailed on A.Y. 2020-21 CHRONOLOGY OF DATES Sr. No. Particulars Date L Due Date for filing Form No. 10B - 31/10/2020 Audit Report 2. Due Date for filing return of income 30/11/2020 u/s.!39(l)of the Act 3. Due Date extended for filing return of 15/02/2021 income as per Press Release dated 30/12/2020 4. Due Date extended for filing the audit 15/01/2021 report as per Press Release dated 30/12/2020 4. Return of income e-filed on 15/02/2021 5. Form No.10B dated 15/02/2021 e-filed 15/02/2021 on 6. No. of days of delay in filing the Form 30 days No.10B. 7. Intimation order u/s. 143(1) of the Act 30//11/2021 5 ITA No.3779-3782/Mum/2024 AL Salam Educational Trust 8. CIT(A) order passed 11/06/2024 9. Application u/s.119(2)(b) of the Act to 04/09/2024 the CIT (Exemption) dated 04/09/2024 filed on 10. Application u/s.119(2)(b) of the Act to 04/09/2024 the CBDT dated 04/09/2024 e-mailed on A.Y. 2021-22 CHRONOLOGY OF DATES Sr. No. Particulars Date 1. Due Date for filing Form No. 10B - 30/09/2021 Audit Report 2. Due Date for filing return of income 31/10/2021 u/s.!39(l)of the Act 3. Due Date extended for filing return of 15/03/2022 income as per Circular No.1 of 2022 [EN0.225/49/2021/ITA-H], dated 11/01/2022 4. Due Date extended for filing the audit 15/02/2022 report 4. Return of income e- filed on 14/03/2022 5. Form No.10B dated 07/03/2022 e-filed 07/03/2022 on 6. No. of days of delay in filing the Form 19 days No.10B. 7. Intimation order u/s. 143(1) of the Act 261 1 10/2022 8. CIT(A) order passed 20/06/2024 6 ITA No.3779-3782/Mum/2024 AL Salam Educational Trust 9. Application u/s.119(2)(b) of the Act to 09/08/2024 the CIT (Exemption) dated 05/08/2024 filed on 10. Application u/s.119(2)(b) of the Act to 20/08/2024 the CBDT dated 05/08/2024 e-mailed on 5. She further submitted that assessee has filed application for condonation of delay before ld. CIT (Exemption) u/s. 119(2)(b) of the Act and also before the CBDT. The reason for delay has been explained under the said condonation of delay and one of the main reasons given that all the accounts were handed by a Trustee Mr. Mahmood Wahab Khan on 13/07/2017 looking after the functioning of the Trust and thereafter, there were disruptions in the management and after his demise, various financial and management difficulties had arisen in the Trust. In all the years assessee had spent the income purely for running of schools and educational activities and all the years audit report has been obtained. There was delay in filing of the audit report, firstly for the reason that there were extended time limits for filing of the return u/s. 139(1) by the CBDT and also due to Covid pandemic times. Further, she submitted that there are umpteen number of judgments wherein it has been held that the filing of audit report u/s.10B alongwith return of income is only directory. Thus, she requested that matter should be restored back to the JAO / AO to allow the exemption and also direction should be given to CIT (Exemption) to condone the delay. 7 ITA No.3779-3782/Mum/2024 AL Salam Educational Trust 6. We have heard both the parties and also perused the relevant facts on record. In all these years, the exemption u/s.11 has been denied by the CPC without affording any opportunity on the ground that there was a delay in filing of Form 10B. The ld. CIT(A) too has passed exparte order on the ground that there was no compliance on the notices sent. The statute has given a time limit for filing of the audit report which is one month prior to the date of filing of return prescribed u/s.139(1) . The power to condone such kind of delay has been provided u/s.119 (2)(b) . The CBDT vide Circular dated 03/01/2020 has directed that, where there is a delay up to 365 days in filing Form 10B for A.Y. 2018-19 or for any subsequent years, the Commissioner of Income Tax is obliged to admit such application for condonation of delay u/s.119(2) of the Act and decide on merits. Again CBDT vide Circular No.16 of 2022 dated 19/07/2022 have extended the period of delay to condone beyond 365 days upto three years in filing of Form 10B for A.Y.2018-19 and for subsequent years, which powers has been given to the Pr. Chief Commissioner and Chief Commissioner to condone the delay and decide on merits. Thus, the statute has given this power for condonation of delay in filing of Form 10B or any audit report u/s. 119(2)(d) to the CITS/CCITs/PCCITs. If the power has been given to condone the delay and various courts have held that it is only directory then under such circumstances such powers needs to be exercised judicially and favorably. In cases of a genuine trust and where there are bonafide and reasonable ground for delay in filing of the audit report in Form 10B, then delay should be condoned 8 ITA No.3779-3782/Mum/2024 AL Salam Educational Trust because CBDT has entrusted such a power to high officials, then they are expected to be more judicious rather than being too stringent even for marginal delays when all judicial courts have held that the purpose of Audit report is that it should be available at the time of filing of return or before the AO before completion of assessment. Accordingly we direct the Ld. CIT Exemption to condone the delay. 7. In so far as power to condone the delay, the same has not been given to Appellate Authorities, or enshrined in section 246 or 246 A or section 253. Other than Section 119(2)(b) , there is no other provision given to any other appellate authority to entertain such appeal or condone the delay. 8. Once Section 119 of the Act has conferred power upon the CBDT to issue instructions and directions to the Income Tax Authorities as it may deem fit for proper administration of the Act which are required to be observed and followed by the Income Tax authorities and CBDT under Sub- section 2 of Section 119 to avoid genuine hardship in the matter of filing of audit report in Form 10B has issued various circulars empowering the authorities at the level of CITs and above to condone the delay in filing of Form 10B for A.Y.2018-19 and subsequent years, then such authorities should condone the delay following the judicial precedents laid down by the various Courts. For instance Hon'ble Gujarat High Court in the case of Sarvodaya Charitable Trust reported in (2021)125 taxmann.com 75 has discussed this issue in detail including 9 ITA No.3779-3782/Mum/2024 AL Salam Educational Trust the CBDT Circular held that approach in such cases should be equitious, balancing and judicious and benefit of exemption should not be denied merely on account of such marginal delay especially in the present case where audit report has been filed alongwith return of income within the due date of Section 139(1) . Similarly, the Hon'ble Jurisdictional High Court in the case of Little Angels Education Society vs. Union of India and Others in Writ Petition No.1061 of 2020 and Writ Petition No.1288 of 2020 vide judgment and order dated 25/03/2021 have also directed to condone the delay even beyond 365 days. Further the Hon'ble Gujarat High Court in the case of Social Security Scheme of GICEA vs. Commissioner of Income Tax (Exemptions ) reported in (2023) 147 taxmann.com 283 also reiterated the same. Thus, power to condone delay should be based on such judicial principles. 9. Accordingly, now that power has been given to ld. CIT (Exemption) by the CBDT and looking to the bonafide and genuine reasons as has been elaborated in the petition for condonation before the CIT(Exemption), we direct CIT (Exemption) to condone the delay by taking a liberal view and decide the petition expeditiously and on such condonation of delay ld. AO is directed to grant exemption in accordance with law. Accordingly, the matter is restored back to the ld. AO who after the petition has been disposed of by the ld. CIT (Exemption), ld. AO should decide in accordance with the law. 10 ITA No.3779-3782/Mum/2024 AL Salam Educational Trust 10. In the result, all the appeals of the assessee are allowed for statistical purposes. Order pronounced on 30th September, 2024. Sd/- Sd/- (RENU JAUHRI) (AMIT SHUKLA) ACCOUNTANT MEMBER JUDICIAL MEMBER Mumbai; Dated 30/ 09/2024 KARUNA, sr.ps Copy of the Order forwarded to : 1. The Appellant 2. The Respondent. 3. CIT 4. DR, ITAT, Mumbai 5. Guard file. //True Copy// BY ORDER, (Asstt. Registrar) ITAT, Mumbai
